key_insight: Random reshuffling provably outperforms standard SGD for smooth convex optimization after any finite number of epochs, removing a longstanding theory-practice gap.
engineering_relevance: This result strengthens the case for using shuffled data passes in large-scale optimization pipelines, especially when data are finite and epoch-level performance matters.
method: Derive convergence guarantees for random reshuffling under smooth convex objectives.
Relax prior stepsize restrictions tied to 1/n thresholds.
Compare finite-epoch behavior against classical SGD to show domination.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports new provable convergence rates and a finite-epoch comparison showing RR dominates SGD under any reasonable stepsize.
watch_item: The guarantee is stated for smooth convex optimization, so applicability outside this setting may require new analysis or tuning.
paper_title: Random Reshuffling Dominates Stochastic Gradient Descent
